notes:
A security-hardening release: the fixes from our 2026-06-20 internal security
audit, closing off device-revocation, login-brute-force, sync-transport, and
content-injection weaknesses across the app, sync server, and feedback worker.

**Fixes & polish**

- **Remote sync now requires HTTPS.** A plaintext `http://` sync server URL is
  refused for any non-loopback host: under server-held key custody a plain
  connection would expose your raw vault key, password, and session token to
  anyone on the network path. If you self-host a remote `http://` server it will
  stop connecting — switch its URL to `https://`. (Local development against
  `localhost`/loopback over `http://` still works.) (#218)
- **Revoking a device now cuts it off immediately.** When you revoke a lost or
  stolen device, its login token stops working at once on every route and it can
  no longer fetch the vault key needed to decrypt your notes. A previously-revoked
  device that was still syncing will be logged out. (#206, #207)
- **Login is now rate-limited.** An attacker can no longer brute-force or
  credential-stuff your password at network speed: too many wrong attempts on an
  email (20 within 5 minutes) return a temporary lockout with a retry-after, and a
  successful login clears the counter. (#208, #210)
- **Pasting an image URL can no longer reach internal or LAN addresses.** Image
  fetches now connect only to public IPs — including across redirects — so a
  pasted URL can't be abused to probe internal services or cloud-metadata
  endpoints (an SSRF guard). (#211)
- **Markdown export no longer crashes on an odd daily-note date.** A malformed or
  non-standard date on a daily note used to abort the whole export; such dates are
  now handled safely and routed aside instead of failing the export. (#212)

**Under the hood**

- **Sync server rejects implausibly-future logical clocks.** A push stamped with
  an HLC set far into the future used to be accepted, which could poison
  last-write-wins so honest devices' edits silently lost forever; such timestamps
  (and malformed HLCs) are now rejected rather than stored. (#214)
- **Title-deduplication loops are now bounded.** Note apply and import cap their
  same-title probing (1000 attempts, then a uuid fallback), so adversarial
  duplicate-title data can't trigger O(n²) blowups. (#213)
- **Feedback worker sanitizes attacker-controlled input.** Submitted fields are
  escaped before they reach GitHub-rendered markdown (no link, heading, details,
  or @mention injection), and screenshots are validated by magic bytes so only
  real PNG/JPEG/WebP/GIF images are committed. (#215, #216)
- **Release-note rendering allowlists link schemes.** Markdown links in the
  auto-updater manifest notes are limited to `http`/`https`/`mailto` and relative
  URLs, so a malicious `data:` or `javascript:` link renders as plain text instead
  of a live anchor. (#217)
- **Feedback submissions can carry an auth token.** The release build now wires
  through `INFINITI_FEEDBACK_TOKEN`, letting the feedback worker require
  authentication when enabled server-side. (#170)
